---
title: "An Automatic Lung Nodule Classification System Based on Hybrid Transfer Learning Approach"
author:
  - Trishna Saikia
  - Ranjeet Kumar
  - Dilip Kumar
  - Koushlendra Kumar Singh
date: "2022-07-01"
slug: lung-nodule-classification
categories:
  - research
  - lung nodule
  - medical imaging
  - transfer learning
  - deep learning
  - healthcare AI
description: "SN Computer Science"
summary: "A hybrid transfer learning-based system for classifying lung nodules from CT scan images into different lung cancer categories."
subtitle: "Hybrid transfer learning for lung nodule classification from CT scan images."
image: featured.jpg
links:
  - icon: file-richtext-fill
    name: Publication
    url: https://doi.org/10.1007/s42979-022-01167-0
  - icon: link-45deg
    name: Springer
    url: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s42979-022-01167-0
---

## Overview

This work presents an automatic lung nodule classification system based on a **hybrid transfer learning approach**. The study focuses on classifying lung-patient CT scan images into four categories: **small-cell carcinoma**, **adenocarcinoma**, **squamous cell carcinoma**, and **large-cell carcinoma**.

The paper was published in **SN Computer Science**, Volume 3, Article 272, in 2022.

## Motivation

Lung cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related mortality worldwide. Early and accurate classification of lung nodules from CT images can support clinical decision-making and assist radiologists in identifying different lung cancer subtypes.

Manual analysis of CT scans is time-consuming and may vary across experts. Therefore, automated computer-aided diagnosis systems based on deep learning and transfer learning can provide useful support for medical image analysis.

## Method

The proposed system uses a **hybrid transfer learning strategy** for lung nodule classification. Transfer learning enables the model to benefit from representations learned by deep convolutional neural networks on large-scale image datasets, which is particularly useful when medical image datasets are limited.

The approach combines feature extraction and classification strategies to improve the representation of CT scan images and classify lung nodules into clinically relevant categories.

## Key Contributions

- Developed an automatic lung nodule classification framework using CT scan images.
- Applied a hybrid transfer learning strategy for medical image classification.
- Classified lung nodules into four lung cancer categories.
- Demonstrated the usefulness of deep learning-based computer-aided diagnosis for lung cancer analysis.
- Contributed to early research experience in medical imaging and healthcare AI.

## Publication

**Trishna Saikia**, Ranjeet Kumar, Dilip Kumar, and Koushlendra Kumar Singh.
“An Automatic Lung Nodule Classification System Based on Hybrid Transfer Learning Approach.”
*SN Computer Science*, Volume 3, Article 272, 2022.

DOI: [10.1007/s42979-022-01167-0](https://doi.org/10.1007/s42979-022-01167-0)
